dtcs.description: 35, C1331/36: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Signal

36 - Open Circuit in Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit The speed sensors detect the wheel speeds and send appropriate signals to the skid control ECU. Speed sensor rotors have rows of alternating N and S magnetic poles, and their magnetic fields change as the rotors turn. The speed sensors detect those magnetic changes and send pulse signals to the skid control ECU. The ECU monitors the wheel speeds through these pulse signals to control the ABS control system. HINT * DTCs C0200/31 and C1330/35 are for the front speed sensor RH. * DTCs C0205/32 and C1331/36 are for the front speed sensor LH. NOTICE: * When replacing the VSC actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. * Check the speed sensor signal in test mode after cleaning or replacement See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 51, C0273/13: Open in Pump Motor Circuit

11 - Open or Short Circuit in ABS Solenoid Relay Circuit The solenoid relay (skid control ECU interior circuit) supplies power to the ABS solenoid and TRC solenoid. After the ignition switch is turned ON, the solenoid is determined to be normal by the initial check self-diagnosis, the relay switches on. If any open or short circuits are detected, the relay switches off. This DTC may be set if the voltage supply to the solenoid relay (+BS) falls below the DTC detection threshold due to the battery or alternator outputs being insufficient. NOTICE: When replacing the VSC actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 41 - Low Battery Positive Voltage or Abnormally High Battery Positive Voltage When there is an abnormality in the power supply circuit of the VSC actuator (skid control ECU), the skid control ECU sets a DTC and the operation is prohibited by the fail-safe function. This DTC is set when the voltage supplied to terminal +BS is outside the DTC detection threshold due to abnormalities of the battery, power source circuits or charging circuits such as the alternator circuit. The fail-safe function is canceled when the voltage to terminal +BS returns to normal. HINT If the vehicle speed is 20 km/h (12 mph) or less when the battery voltage is low, the ABS warning light, VSC OFF indicator light and SLIP indicator light illuminate. However, a DTC will not be output. dtcs.description: 73 - Engine or Transmission System Matching Error This DTC is set by the skid control ECU when the ECM is replaced with one that has different engine and transmission information than the information stored in the skid control ECU. This DTC is also set when the VSC actuator assembly (skid control ECU) is replaced with one that has different engine and transmission information than the information stored in the ECM. The ECM determines the transmission information according to whether or not there is a connection at a certain ECM terminal and sends its stored engine and transmission information. NOTICE: * When replacing the VSC actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. * for 1GR-FE: If the ECM has been replaced, it is necessary to register the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * for 1UR-FE: If the ECM has been replaced, it is necessary to register the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * for 3UR-FE: If the ECM has been replaced, it is necessary to register the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * for 3UR-FBE: If the ECM has been replaced, it is necessary to register the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 66, C1445/66: Steering Angle Sensor Initialization Incomplete

66 - Vehicle Driven with Steering Angle Sensor not Initialized The skid control ECU acquires the steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for approximately 10 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. The steering angle sensor zero point malfunction warning will be canceled by turning the ignition switch off. NOTICE: * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the output DTC is related to temporary loss of the steering angle zero point calibration data, such as when the power source voltage drops when the battery is removed, and confirm whether this condition occurred. * When U0073/94, U0123/62, U0124/69 and/or U0126/63 is output together with C1231/31, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0073/94, U0123/62, U0124/69 and/or U0126/63 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and deceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and deceleration sensor are output together with DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and deceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Vehicle Stability Control System. * When the steering angle sensor and/or yaw rate and deceleration sensor is replaced, perform zero point calibration for the applicable sensor(s) See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. * When replacing the VSC actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON again and check that no CAN communication system DTC is output. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 36 km/h (22 mph), turn the steering wheel to the right and left and check that no speed sensor or yaw rate and deceleration sensor DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT * If there is a malfunction in the speed sensor or the yaw rate and deceleration sensor, a DTC may be output although the steering angle sensor is normal. * If the speed sensor and the yaw rate and deceleration sensor DTCs are output simultaneously, repair the sensors and then inspect the steering angle sensor. * If the same DTC (C1439/66 and/or C1445/66) is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero point calibration data, such as when the power source voltage drops. D --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS (SYMPTOM SIMULATION)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting C --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Vehicle Stability Control System B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/CAN Communication System A -- Continue to next step.

(b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If the front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and deceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If the same (DTC C1439/66 and/or C1445/66) is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero point calibration, such as when the power source voltage drops. B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR (a) Replace the steering angle sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Steering Angle Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for at least 10 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If the front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and deceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (22 mph) or more and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. dtcs.description: 21-C2124/24, C2181/81-C2184/84: No Signal from Transmitter ID1

84 - Transmitter ID4 not Received (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning valves and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 41-C2144/44: Transmitter ID1 Error

44 - Transmitter ID4 Error When the internal circuit of a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is malfunctioning, one of these DTCs is output. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch to ON and the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Display the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the tester. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data displayed on the tester (ID1 to ID4) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data is the cause of the output DTC. HINT * Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. * Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) corresponds to which tire. (h) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take up to about 5 to 6 minutes to update the tire pressure data. If the values are not displayed after about 5 to 6 minutes, perform troubleshooting according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2124/24    See: C2121. Result NOTICE: * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (i) When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) has changed, repeat this procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. (j) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using recorded transmitter IDs and the output DTC. (k) Set the tire pressures to the appropriate specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The transmitter IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter ID for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 65-C2168/68: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire

68 - Abnormal Temperature Inside ID4 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as the tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction, outputs DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

HINT If a tire is damaged, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter may also have been damaged at the same time. NG --  REPLACE TIRE AND TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER OK -- Continue to next step.
IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC   See: C2141 NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The transmitter IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter ID for all 4 tires    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The transmitter IDs of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter are registered to the tire pressure warning ECU. When the IDs have never been registered, a DTC is output. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED TRANSMITTER IDS) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The registered transmitter IDs are displayed on the Techstream screen. NG --  PERFORM REGISTRATION (TRANSMITTER ID) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

PERFORM REGISTRATION (TRANSMITTER ID) (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 74, C2191/91: Vehicle Speed Signal Malfunction

91 is stored only during the signal check. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. HINT When the speedometer needle is not moving, first troubleshoot the meter/gauge system See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Meter / Gauge System/How To Proceed With . PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING DATA LIST (VEHICLE SPEED) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (e) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: Vehicle speed indicated on the Techstream indicates the actual speed. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - COMBINATION METER) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Perform a driving test. (b) Check for DTCs. HINT The time required for DTC C2174 to be stored is 20 minutes. Result B --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - COMBINATION METER) (a) Disconnect the J31 tire pressure warning ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the J28 meter connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER - SKID CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the A17 skid control ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the J28 meter connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (SFI SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTCs. HINT * for 1GR-FE    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. * for 1UR-FE    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. * for 3UR-FE    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. * for 3UR-FBE    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Combination Meter/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (a) Replace the tire pressure warning ECU    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Perform registration    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Perform a driving test. (b) Check for DTCs. HINT The time required for DTC C2174 to be stored is 20 minutes. Result B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 76 - Receiver Error The signals are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTC. Result B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 77 - Initialization not Completed Initialization is necessary if one of the following occurs: 1) the ECU is replaced; 2) the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is replaced; 3) tires with a different tire standard pressure are installed; 4) the tires are rotated; or 5) a new vehicle is delivered. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check if the vehicle is in conditions such as the ones described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may blink for a minute and turn on in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities, or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT Radio frequency may be interrupted due to surroundings or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  GO TO C2121/21 TO C2124/24 FLOWCHART  See: C2121

dtcs.description: 78 - Battery Voltage Malfunction If DTC C2178/78 is output, inspect the battery and charging system for 1GR-FE See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection, 1UR-FE See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection, 3UR-FE See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection, or 3UR-FBE See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection. If the battery and charging system are normal, perform the "ECU Power Source Circuit" inspection procedure See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/ECU Power Source Circuit.
